quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 14 Quiz 5
quiz.subtitle: 14.5: Determinants of 2x2 and 3x3 Matrices
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Calculating the scalar value of a square matrix

questions.question: Find the determinant of $\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 0 & 1 & 4 \\ 1 & 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$ by expanding along the first row.
Use the Rule of Sarrus to find $\det\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 3 & 0 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \end{bmatrix}$.
Find the determinant of $\begin{bmatrix} 3 & 5 & 2 \\ 0 & 4 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 6 \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Triangular matrix: determinant = product of diagonal entries. $3 \times 4 \times 6 = 72$.

questions.question: Given $\det\begin{bmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{bmatrix} = 8$, find $\det\begin{bmatrix} 2a & 2b \\ c & d \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Multiplying a single row by $k$ multiplies the determinant by $k$. New $\det = 2 \times 8 = 16$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You are proficient in determinants — 2×2, 3×3 expansion by minors, Sarrus' rule, triangular matrices, solving for unknowns, and applying determinant properties.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Key reminders: (1) $\det(A) = ad - bc$ for 2×2, (2) Expansion by minors uses the sign pattern $+ - +$ / $- + -$ / $+ - +$, (3) $\det(AB) = \det(A)\det(B)$, (4) Triangular matrix determinant = product of diagonal entries.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Signs when expanding 3×3 — easy to drop a negative, (2) Sarrus: sum three downward products, subtract three upward products, (3) Multiplying a row by $k$ multiplies $\det$ by $k$, (4) Swapping rows flips the sign of the determinant.
scoreMessages.0: Review determinants: (1) 2×2: $\det = ad - bc$, (2) 3×3 expansion by minors: choose a row, use sign pattern, compute 2×2 minors, (3) Sarrus' rule: repeat first two columns, three downward products minus three upward products, (4) $\det(AB) = \det(A)\det(B)$, (5) Triangular matrix: product of diagonal entries.
